use_cases.short_description: High-frequency semantic product search using query embeddings with structured filters (category, price, availability) under strict latency SLAs at very large scale.
decision_factors: Projected growth toward billion-plus representations
High concurrency with strict p95/p99 latency requirements
Vector similarity combined with structured filters
Potential need for keyword + semantic fusion
use_cases.growth_note: Projected billion-scale growth and rising concurrency make hyperscale vector architecture mandatory to avoid future migration complexity.
use_cases.why_chosen: Hybrid index enables hyperscale ANN performance with structured and keyword filtering under strict latency constraints.

why_not.HVI: Does not natively handle advanced keyword scoring and hybrid ranking requirements at ecommerce scale.
why_not.CVI: Not enough selectivity and does not support keyword search
why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: Does not support vector search at billion scale

use_cases.short_description: User-personalized recommendation retrieval using stored user embeddings to perform vector similarity search against product embeddings with business filters.
decision_factors: 300M+ product vector corpus
Vector only search needed at that scale
Strict tail-latency requirements under high concurrency
ANN-first retrieval with lightweight business filters
Large candidate retrieval (top-k up to 200)
use_cases.growth_note: As recommendation traffic and corpus size grow toward billion-scale, hyperscale ANN architecture ensures predictable latency.
use_cases.why_chosen: Hyperscale Vector Index provides optimal ANN performance and scalability for pure vector recommendation workloads.

why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: FTS_Vector_Search does not perform well at 300M scale
why_not.Hybrid: We do not need keyword search and hybrid adds keyword overhead that is unnecessary for pure embedding-driven recommendations.

use_cases.short_description: Category-first navigation with structured attribute filters and optional semantic refinement within a constrained product subset.
decision_factors: High-selectivity category and attribute filters
Structured browsing combined with semantic refinement
Pre-filtering reduces search space to less than 20%
Massive product corpus
use_cases.growth_note: Composite can handle billion scale here effectively because the filter is only 10% selective, meaning 90% of the corpus is eliminated before ANN runs.
use_cases.why_chosen: Composite benefits directly from a 10% selective filter — the GSI pre-filter eliminates 90% of the ecommerce catalog before the ANN step, dramatically shrinking the search space.

why_not.HVI: Does not work better than composite when filters reduce the search space to less than 20% of the data
why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: FTS_Vector_Search alone cannot deliver high-quality semantic similarity ranking.
why_not.Hybrid: Hybrid adds keyword overhead that is not needed for pure embedding-driven recommendations.

use_cases.short_description: Real-time personalized content retrieval where a user/session embedding is used to retrieve top-k most similar content vectors from a massive and rapidly growing corpus under strict tail-latency constraints. Uses filters like Content freshness, language or reginon, safety and policy constraints.
decision_factors: Initial deployment already at 150-300M content vectors with projection beyond 1B within 24-36 months
Hundreds of thousands of vector queries per second with very high concurrency
Strict p95 (<100ms) and tightly bounded p99 latency requirements
Vector similarity is first-stage retrieval over very large candidate pool
Filters (freshness, language, safety) are low-to-medium selectivity and do not drastically shrink ANN search space
Frequent user vector updates and continuous content ingestion
use_cases.why_chosen: HVI is purpose-built for hyperscale ANN workloads with billion-scale growth, high QPS, and strict tail-latency guarantees.

why_not.CVI: CVI performs really bad at that low level of selectivity when compared to hybrid
why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: FTS_Vector_Search cannot sustain billion-scale ANN workloads with predictable latency under extreme QPS.
why_not.Hybrid: Hybrid adds unnecessary keyword infrastructure overhead that is not needed since the dominant retrieval pattern is pure embedding similarity with lightweight filters.

use_cases.short_description: Real-time AI-driven job search and recommendation engine for billions of job vectors (similar to LinkedIN), supporting low-selectivity filters and instant personalized alerts.
decision_factors: Dataset exceeds 1 billion job vectors and continues to grow rapidly
Low-selectivity filters (role, location, salary) eliminate only a small portion of vectors
Strict latency requirements for personalized recommendations and instant job alerts
Continuous ingestion of new job postings and frequent updates to job embeddings
High uptime and reliability requirements for mission-critical search
use_cases.why_chosen: HVI is optimized for hyperscale ANN workloads, supporting billion-plus vectors with strict low-latency guarantees under low-selectivity filtering.

why_not.CVI: Composite index cannot efficiently handle billion-scale vectors with a 70% selective filter — when 70% of the corpus is eligible for vector search, the GSI pre-filter barely reduces the ANN search space, wasting RAM for little gain.
why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: Full-text search lacks ANN efficiency and cannot sustain sub-20ms tail latency for billions of vectors.
why_not.Hybrid: Hybrid adds unnecessary keyword ranking overhead, while the dominant workload is pure vector similarity with low-selectivity filters.

use_cases.short_description: Hybrid product search for a D2C ecommerce platform, combining text and vector search to handle natural language queries, typos, and autocomplete for eco-friendly products.
decision_factors: Hybrid search combining text and vector similarity to handle natural language queries
Autocomplete and fuzzy matching to support typos and improve discovery
Moderate scale (~30 QPS) where performance is secondary to relevance
Filters (category, product attributes) are manageable and well supported by FTS_Vector_Search
Vector similarity is used as a secondary signal for semantic relevance
use_cases.why_chosen: FTS_Vector_Search provides native support for hybrid text + vector search, autocomplete, and fuzzy matching at moderate scale with filtered search.

why_not.HVI: Overkill for moderate-scale workload and adds unnecessary complexity for text search features.
why_not.CVI: Does not natively support hybrid text + vector retrieval or fuzzy matching.
why_not.Hybrid: Hybrid index is unnecessary given the low QPS and manageable dataset scale; FTS_Vector_Search suffices.

use_cases.short_description: Complex real estate search with multi-layered filters on location, price, amenities, and property type, where the filter is approximately 8% selective (only 8% of listings are eligible for vector search) over 40M listings.
decision_factors: Highly selective filters across multiple dimensions (location, price, amenities, property type)
Integration with existing SQL-based workflows in Couchbase
Moderate scale (40M listings) with peak 200 QPS
Performance and relevance prioritized over pure ANN throughput
Filters dramatically reduce the candidate search space
use_cases.why_chosen: CVI supports advanced, high-selectivity filters efficiently while maintaining predictable latency for real-time search over moderate-scale datasets.

why_not.HVI: HVI is overkill for a moderate-scale workload where the filter is already 8% selective — CVI's pre-filter eliminates 92% of the corpus before ANN, which is exactly the scenario CVI is optimized for.
why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: Full-text search cannot efficiently handle complex, multi-dimensional vector filters.
why_not.Hybrid: Hybrid adds unnecessary complexity; CVI alone satisfies high-selectivity filtering needs.

use_cases.short_description: Strictly isolated semantic search for enterprise documents in a multi-tenant SaaS platform.
decision_factors: Tenant isolation requires extremely selective pre-filtering per query
Query volume is high but each tenant only touches a small subset of vectors
Total corpus is massive with projections to exceed 1B vectors
Strict tail-latency requirements (<20ms) for enterprise SLA compliance
Frequent updates across tenants necessitate efficient, selective vector indexing
use_cases.why_chosen: CVI provides the necessary pre-filtering to ensure recall stability and performance at 0.01% selectivity.

why_not.HVI: Low selectivity causes HVI to probe too many centroids, leading to worse latency.
why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: Search Vector Service would breach its limit and consume excessive RAM leading to high latency.
why_not.Hybrid: Over-engineered for a requirement focused purely on tenant-filtered vector similarity.

use_cases.short_description: Hybrid search for a national identity system requiring both keyword and vector similarity search over 4B records, supporting fast, accurate retrieval and filtering.
decision_factors: Dataset contains 4B identity records requiring high-scale vector search
Requires hybrid queries combining keyword search (names, addresses, IDs) with semantic vector search (biometric embeddings, document embeddings)
Low-selectivity filters (region, age, enrollment center) do not drastically reduce search space
Strict latency requirements for verification and retrieval of identity records
Frequent updates for new enrollments, corrections, and periodic re-embedding for biometric models
use_cases.why_chosen: Hybrid index combines hyperscale ANN with keyword filtering to efficiently support billion-scale vector + text search under strict latency requirements.

why_not.HVI: HVI alone cannot efficiently support keyword-based search and filtering across 4B records.
why_not.CVI: CVI is unsuitable at this scale and low filter selectivity; RAM cost would be prohibitive.
why_not.FTS_Vector_Search: FTS cannot deliver billion-scale vector similarity with strict latency requirements.

use_cases.short_description: Genetic profile matching for a small-scale cancer research dataset.
decision_factors: Dataset is small (<1M vectors) and does not require scaling
No filtering or keywords are necessary
Query latency is flexible, allowing simple index structures
Minimal operational overhead is preferred for research workloads
use_cases.growth_note: Stable growth ensures the exact matching approach will never hit a performance wall.
use_cases.why_chosen: FTS/Search Vector handles small datasets with minimal configuration overhead.

why_not.HVI: HVI is designed for approximate search; At this scale fts vector search is easier and manageable
why_not.CVI: No scalar filters exist to justify the complexity of a Composite index at this small scale.
why_not.Hybrid: Both HVI(optimized to handle billion scale) and Keyword search is not required for genetic sequence matching.

use_cases.short_description: Tenant-isolated semantic document search for a SaaS knowledge platform where every query is restricted to a specific tenant subset.
decision_factors: Each query is strictly scoped to one tenant (5K-50K docs per tenant out of 80M total)
Tenant filter eliminates well over 80% of global corpus before ANN search
Additional metadata filters (document type, access level) further reduce search space
Latency targets (p50 <40ms, p95 <100ms) require predictable filtered ANN performance
Top-k is small (10), favoring efficient pre-filtered candidate generation
Moderate update rate with continuous document ingestion
use_cases.why_chosen: CVI benefits from extremely high tenant-level selectivity, dramatically shrinking the ANN search space and ensuring stable, predictable latency.

why_not.HVI: HVI is optimized for low-selectivity global ANN search and does not leverage strong tenant pre-filtering.
why_not.FTS: FTS is not optimized for large-scale filtered vector workloads with strict tenant isolation.
why_not.Hybrid: Hybrid adds unnecessary keyword search complexity when the dominant requirement is filtered semantic similarity.
